body.title: Standardize timestamp formats across Igor and agent-datacenter
body.result: Created ts_format.py (canonical helper: format_display/format_slug/parse_iso). export_chat.py now shows local YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS in message headers + footer. Machine-to-machine paths unchanged.
body.description:
Timestamp formats are inconsistent across projects: /export-chat uses UTC ISO 8601, while most of Igor and agent-datacenter use local time with compact formats (yyyymmdd, yyyymmddhhmmss, hhmm). Standardize on one format (likely local time + compact for web compatibility, e.g. yyyymmdd-hhmm or yyyymmdd_hhmmss) to reduce confusion and simplify auditing across both projects.

**Affected files:** igor/tools (export_chat.py, ops.py, paths.py), agent_datacenter/*, lab/claudecode/ utilities, any code that reads/writes/displays timestamps
**Design rules:** docs-in-code — when standardizing, document the canonical format at the top of any utility that reads/writes timestamps
**Scope boundary:** IN: format audit, selection of standard, code update to use it. OUT: historical log migration, changing actual timestamp values
**Test plan:** audit: find all timestamp format strings in codebase (grep for iso, strftime, etc); no automated tests needed — verify manually that web view and log output use the chosen format consistently after changes
